title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Constable Craddock interrogates the photographer Margaret Bunsen (also referred to as Miss Maple or Miss Benes) regarding her past. After discussing her professional training under Andrew Quill at the Reingold Film Company and her residence in Seven Quays, Craddock confronts her with the fact that she was adopted by Marina Gregg and lived with her for four years.

Margaret admits she was one of three children Marina adopted, alongside an infant named Rod and an older boy named Angus. She describes their life in America as a deceptive performance where Marina acted as a mother for her public image while treating the children as temporary substitutes. Margaret explains that this arrangement ended when Marina became pregnant with her own biological child, who was born with a disability. Margaret expresses deep resentment toward Marina, claiming Marina only made the children feel loved to discard them once her biological child arrived. She notes that Rod moved to the American Midwest to run a farm, while the whereabouts of Angus, who had once expressed a desire to kill the unborn baby, remain unknown.

Margaret clarifies that she traveled to Britain to photograph Marina out of curiosity rather than any prior arrangement. When Craddock questions if she attempted to poison Marina, she denies it. However, Craddock reveals that Marina Gregg is still alive, suggesting that while Margaret did not kill her, a murder has indeed been committed by someone else.
